next_steps: Perform serum and urine immunofixation electrophoresis and serum free light chain assay to rule out AL amyloidosis.
Order Technetium-99m pyrophosphate (PYP) or DPD scintigraphy to evaluate for TTR cardiac amyloidosis.
Obtain alpha-galactosidase A enzyme levels and GLA gene testing for Fabry disease.
Biopsy of skin angiomas (if present) for histopathology and Congo red staining.
Laryngoscopy to evaluate for vocal cord infiltration or nerve dysfunction contributing to hoarseness.
Cardiac biomarkers (NT-proBNP and high-sensitivity troponin) to risk-stratify.
Avoid starting steroids until an inflammatory etiology like sarcoidosis is definitively confirmed.
